_description: Python DB Write Allowlist — historical baseline. ALLOWLIST DOES NOT AUTHORIZE RUNTIME DB WRITES. Each entry is a pre-existing file with DB write risk that was classified in python_sql_migration_enforcement_design_phase1. New or modified Python files with DB write signals that are NOT in this allowlist will fail CI changed-files enforcement.
_runtime_guard_status: PARTIALLY IMPLEMENTED — Phase2C batch1+batch2+batch3: 9 of 14 confirmed write paths have runtime guard; 5 remaining classified by design phase batch4 (2 read_only_candidate, 3 infrastructure_only_needs_caller_guard); Phase2 indirect_write_path_guard_phase2: 6 of 6 indirect write paths now runtime guarded; Phase2D manual review: 5 of 5 manual review candidates classified (2 write_needs_guard, 1 read_only, 2 false_positive). Total: 15/20 runtime guarded, 2 next guard candidates (reprocess_from_local.py, prometheus_metrics.py), 3 safe reclassified. No remaining manual review candidates.

entries.reason: Design phase batch4 analysis: SQL query string store (V4.25 'SQL 弹药库'). Defines SQL string constants (INSERT_MATCH, INSERT_MATCH_MAPPING, UPSERT_RAW_DATA, UPDATE_XG, UPDATE_STATS, UPDATE_L3_ODDS_DATA, UPDATE_GOLDEN_FEATURES, MARK_HARVESTED) but contains NO execution code. No psycopg2/asyncpg/sqlalchemy import. No connect/cursor/execute/commit/rollback. Consumers execute these strings elsewhere. No active consumers found in current codebase scan.
entries.evidence: SQL string constants only in SQLStore class. Zero execution code. No DB driver imports. Write-capable SQL strings exist (INSERT/UPDATE/UPSERT) but are only strings, not executed here. No consumers importing SQLStore found in src/ or scripts/. See docs/SC002_PHASE2C_REMAINING_CONFIRMED_WRITE_PATHS_DESIGN.md #3.
observed_operations: NONE — SQL string constants only, no execution code
entries.direct_write_boundary: NONE — file only defines SQL strings; execution happens in consumer files
entries.recommended_next_action: Consider deprecation/removal in cleanup PR. No guard needed — no consumers to guard.
entries.why_not_guarded_in_this_pr: Audit confirmed zero active consumers. No runtime guard needed at infrastructure level or consumer level.
entries.expires_or_review_note: Reclassified from confirmed_write_path to infrastructure_only_needs_caller_guard by design phase batch4 (2026-06-24). Guard at consumers, not here.
entries.consumer_summary: Zero active consumers. SQLStore class has no external imports or usages. SQL string constants exist but no code executes them.

entries.reason: Design phase batch4 analysis: sync PostgreSQL connection pool infrastructure (psycopg2.pool.ThreadedConnectionPool). execute() and executemany() methods execute arbitrary SQL with auto-commit (active_conn.commit()). These methods serve BOTH read and write — they cannot distinguish INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE from SET/health-check operations. Guard at this level would be overly broad and break read-only consumers. Fetch methods (fetch_all, fetch_one) are read-only and need no guard.
entries.evidence: psycopg2 + ThreadedConnectionPool + execute/executemany with active_conn.commit(). Generic SQL execution — no DML/DDL distinction. Callers pass arbitrary SQL. Consumer: src/utils/__init__.py imports get_sync_db_pool. Convenience functions re-exported via db_pool.py. See docs/SC002_PHASE2C_REMAINING_CONFIRMED_WRITE_PATHS_DESIGN.md #4.
observed_tables_or_targets: N/A — infrastructure, tables depend on caller's SQL
entries.direct_write_boundary: YES — execute()/executemany() with auto-commit. But guard at infrastructure level is inappropriate: methods serve both read+write; guard would break health checks and read-only callers.
entries.recommended_next_action: Consider removing unused utils aliases. No guard needed — no active write consumers.
entries.why_not_guarded_in_this_pr: Audit confirmed zero active write consumers of SyncDatabasePool. All write consumers use async DatabasePool and are already guarded.
entries.expires_or_review_note: Reclassified from confirmed_write_path to infrastructure_only_needs_caller_guard by design phase batch4 (2026-06-24). Guard at consumers, not here.
entries.consumer_summary: One consumer (src/utils/__init__.py) re-exports SyncDatabasePool as DatabaseManager with zero downstream consumers. Remaining consumers use async DatabasePool instead.

entries.reason: Design phase batch4 analysis: async PostgreSQL connection pool infrastructure (asyncpg). execute() and executemany() methods execute arbitrary SQL via asyncpg (auto-commit by default). These methods serve BOTH read and write — they cannot distinguish DML/DDL from health-check operations. Fetch methods (fetch, fetchrow, fetchval) are read-only. Also re-exports sync_db_pool convenience functions. Two known write callers (collector_repository.py, streaming_db_writer.py) already guarded in batch3. Other callers (main.py, health.py, monitoring.py) appear read-only.
entries.evidence: asyncpg + Pool + execute/executemany (auto-commit). Generic SQL execution — no DML/DDL distinction. Known callers: src/main.py (bootstrap), src/api/health.py (health checks, SELECT), src/api/monitoring.py (monitoring), collector_repository.py (write, batch3 guarded), streaming_db_writer.py (write, batch3 guarded). See docs/SC002_PHASE2C_REMAINING_CONFIRMED_WRITE_PATHS_DESIGN.md #5.
observed_tables_or_targets: N/A — infrastructure, tables depend on caller's SQL
entries.direct_write_boundary: YES — execute()/executemany() with asyncpg auto-commit. But guard at infrastructure level is inappropriate: methods serve both read+write; guard would break health checks and read-only consumers.
entries.recommended_next_action: No new guard needed. All write consumers already guarded (collector_repository.py, streaming_db_writer.py in batch3). Read-only consumers verified safe.
entries.why_not_guarded_in_this_pr: Audit confirmed all write consumers already guarded. Read-only consumers verified via static analysis. Infrastructure-level guard inappropriate (would break health checks).
entries.expires_or_review_note: Reclassified from confirmed_write_path to infrastructure_only_needs_caller_guard by design phase batch4 (2026-06-24). Guard at consumers, not here. Two known write callers already guarded in batch3.
entries.consumer_summary: 9 consumers: 2 write (both already guarded in batch3), 6 read-only, 1 test mock. No unguarded write consumers found.

entries.reason: Design phase batch4 analysis: all cursor.execute() calls are SELECT only. check_coverage_gaps(), check_missing_opening(), check_anomalies() — all SELECT-based diagnostic queries. The _generate_cleanup_sql() method constructs a DELETE SQL string but only PRINTS it (console/rich output) — never executes via cursor.execute(). --show-sql-only flag skips DB connection entirely.
entries.evidence: psycopg2 + cursor.execute() — SELECT queries only on matches, match_odds. DELETE SQL in _generate_cleanup_sql() is print-to-console diagnostic, not executed. See docs/SC002_PHASE2C_REMAINING_CONFIRMED_WRITE_PATHS_DESIGN.md #1.
entries.direct_write_boundary: NONE — all cursor.execute() calls are SELECT; DELETE is print-only diagnostic, never executed
entries.recommended_next_action: No guard needed. Reclassify as read-only. If future version adds automated DELETE/UPDATE execution, re-evaluate with a new design task.
entries.why_not_guarded_in_this_pr: Read-only at runtime — no write path to guard. DELETE SQL is only generated and printed, never executed.
entries.expires_or_review_note: Reclassified from confirmed_write_path to read_only_candidate by design phase batch4 (2026-06-24). Review if file is modified to add actual write execution.

entries.reason: Indirect write path design phase1: FALSE POSITIVE — skeleton class with only connection management (_get_connection, close). Zero write methods. Zero INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE.
